target.title: [codex] fix(telegram): sync native commands to private and group scopes [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram native command menus were synced and explicitly cleared for default/group scopes, while private-chat scope could drift or retain stale entries.
- Why it matters: users can see different slash-command menus depending on whether they interact with the bot in DMs, private chats, or groups.
- What changed: register/delete menu commands through default, `all_private_chats`, and `all_group_chats`; clear all three scopes when native commands are disabled; add regression coverage for scope ordering, hash skips, empty menus, and retry behavior.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): command construction, command authorization, Telegram transport behavior, and live Bot API configuration are unchanged.

AI-assisted by **Codex GPT-5.5**.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Telegram command-menu scope handling was added incrementally and did not include private-chat scope everywhere command menus are registered or cleared.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered default/group behavior but did not assert private-chat registration, empty-menu cleanup, or native-disabled cleanup.
- Contributing context (if known): Telegram Bot API command scopes are independent, so a successful default/group sync does not guarantee private-chat menu state is current.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-command-menu.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: all menu sync and cleanup paths cover default, private-chat, and group-chat scopes while preserving hash-cache skip and `BOT_COMMANDS_TOO_MUCH` retry behavior.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the behavior is encoded in local Bot API call shape and ordering, so mocked Bot API calls cover the regression without requiring live Telegram credentials.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing default/group tests were updated to include private-chat scope.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Telegram native slash-command menus are now registered and cleared consistently for private chats and group chats. No config or migration is required.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

```text
Before:
[startup sync] -> [default/group command scopes updated]
[private chat] -> [may keep stale native menu]

After:
[startup sync] -> [default/private/group command scopes updated]
[private chat] -> [same native menu contract as other scopes]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: Node v24.14.1, pnpm 10.33.2
- Model/provider: Codex GPT-5.5 for implementation/review assistance
- Integration/channel (if any): Telegram
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A; tests use mocked Bot API calls

### Steps

1. Configure Telegram native commands with commands to register.
2. Start the Telegram bot or run command-menu sync.
3. Observe Bot API calls for default, private-chat, and group-chat scopes.

### Expected

- `deleteMyCommands` runs before `setMyCommands` for all three scopes.
- `setMyCommands` registers commands for default, `all_private_chats`, and `all_group_chats`.
- Empty command lists delete all scopes and do not set commands when `deleteMyCommands` is available.
- Explicit native-disabled cleanup clears all three scopes.

### Actual

- Before this PR, private-chat scope was not covered consistently by tests or explicit cleanup.
- After this PR, tests assert all three scopes.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Validation run locally:

- `pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-command-menu.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts` — passed, 2 files / 88 tests.
- `pnpm test:extension telegram` — passed, 104 files / 1565 tests.
- `pnpm build` — passed.
- `pnpm check` — passed.
- `codex review --base origin/main` — passed; no actionable correctness issues.
- `pnpm test` — attempted; unrelated local environment failures:
  - `src/gateway/server.canvas-auth.test.ts` cannot bind IPv6 loopback (`EADDRNOTAVAIL ::1`); standalone `net.listen(0, "::1")` also fails on this host.
  - `test/scripts/postinstall-bundled-plugins.test.ts` observes a warning from a root-owned `/tmp/node-compile-cache/v24.14.1-x64-cf738c9d-0` directory.
  - `src/logging/diagnostic-support-export.test.ts` and `src/logging/diagnostic-stability-bundle.test.ts` compare against `os.hostname()`; this host's name is `linux`, which also appears as the platform string.

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: all-scope registration ordering, unchanged-hash skip, empty-menu cleanup, retry shrink behavior, and native-disabled cleanup through targeted tests.
- Edge cases checked: delete failure does not cache empty-menu hash; `BOT_COMMANDS_TOO_MUCH` retry reuses the reduced list across private/group scopes; second empty sync proves async completion before asserting no sets.
- What you did **not** verify: live Telegram Bot API behavior against a real bot token.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

No bot review conversations exist yet for this PR.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: registering one extra Telegram command scope adds another startup Bot API control call.
  - Mitigation: existing bounded logging/error handling and retry behavior are reused for every scope.
